subject: Fw: To Dr Katz, March of Dimes Senor VP for Research & Global Programs
body:
thought you might find  interesting.

----- Forwarded Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Thursday, February 1, 2007 8:48:33 AM
Subject: FW: To Dr Katz, March of Dimes Senor VP for Research & Global Programs

To Dr Katz, March of Dimes Senor VP for Research & Global Programs

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Lesley Groff

Executive Assistant to Jeffrey 
Epstein

 

 




From: Tramo, Mark Jude 
[mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Wednesday, January 31, 2007 
5:08 PM
To: Katz, Michael
Subject: To Dr Katz, March of 
Dimes Senor VP for Research & Global Programs





-----Original Message-----
From:   Katz, Michael 
[mailto:MKatz@marchofdimes.com]
Sent:   
Wed 1/31/2007 2:36 PM
To:     Tramo, Mark 
Jude
Cc:    
Subject:       

Dear 
Dr. Tramo:

 I just viewed your web page and sent it also to my wife. 
This is very
interesting to me and I will continue to follow it. (This does 
not mean
- as I must inform you - that we will give you any preference in 
our
review process, but I am sure that you know this.)

 We will 
look forward to your application in the next 
cycle.

 Regards,

 MK
Michael Katz, MD
Senior Vice 
President for Research
and Global Programs
March of Dimes Birth Defects 
Foundation
(Carpentier Professor, emeritus, of Pediatrics
and Professor, 
emeritus of Public Health,
Columbia University)
1275 Mamaroneck 
Avenue
White Plains, NY 10605
USA
Tel.: 914 997-4555
Fax.: 914 
997-4560
e-mail: mkatz@marchofdimes.com <mailto:mkatz@marchofdimes.com>

*******************************************
Dear 
Dr Katz,

Thank you for your emai and time discussing our application 
today.

In light of your and your wife's interests in biomusicology, I've 
attached the Society for Neuroscience's recent press release about out pilot 
work with premature infants, which we presented at the SFN's Annual Meeting 
several months.  Also attached is a JAMA article about our newly founded 
Institute.

I very much enjoyed our conversation today - - perhaps your 16 
yr-old (and his rock'n'roll bandmates) might enjoy some of the Selected Readings 
downloadable via the Educational Activities link - especially if they know 
George Martin, The Beatles' producer, is one of iMABS' founding Board members 
(and I a rock "retiree").  Then again, given the generational distance, 
perhaps our genre is now considered "classical" and not hard(ly) rock to his 
"ears" (i.e., brain!)

Thanks again for taking the time to explain the 
application process and the positive response to our Letter of Intent.  I 
understand, of course, that your and Mrs Katz's intellectual interests in music, 
medicine, and science run orthogonal to the route through which we 
met. 

Re: you Global Programs - Might you know of other medical 
centers in the U.S. or around the world that might like to partner with us in a 
multi-center randomized controlled clinical trial on auditory enrichment in 
premature infants?

My colleagues and I look forward to uploading our LOI 
for future consideration in the months ahead.

Best 
regards,
Mark

Mark Jude Tramo, MD, PhD
Director, The Institute for 
Music & Brain Science
Dept of Neurology, Harvard Medical School & 
Massachusetts General Hospital
Steering Committee, Harvard University 
Mind/Brain/Behavior Interfaculty Initiative
Best Doctors in America®/ 
America’s Top Physicians®/Best of Boston®
Songwriter Member, 
ASCAP

Mailing Address:  175 Cambridge St, Suite 340, Boston, MA 
02114 USA
Email  MTramo@HMS.Harvard.edu
URL  http://www.BrainMusic.org
Office 
phone  617-724-3992
Cellphone (24/7)  781-718-6390
Fax 
781-990-1397
Page Operator (for call back)  
617-726-2066

Cc:
iMABS Board
Jonathan Cronin, MD
  Assoc 
Med Director, MGH NICU
Peggy Settle, RN
  Nurse Manager, MGH 
NICU












 
____________________________________________________________________________________
Need Mail bonding?
Go to the Yahoo! Mail Q&A for great tips from Yahoo! Answers users.
http://answers.yahoo.com/dir/?link=list&sid=396546091

Dear Dr Katz,

Thank you for your emai and time discussing our application today.

In light of your and your wife's interests in biomusicology, I've attached the Society for Neuroscience's recent press release about out pilot work with premature infants, which we presented at the SFN's Annual Meeting several months.  Also attached is a JAMA article about our newly founded Institute.

I very much enjoyed our conversation today - - perhaps your 16 yr-old (and his rock'n'roll bandmates) might enjoy some of the Selected Readings downloadable via the Educational Activities link - especially if they know George Martin, The Beatles' producer, is one of iMABS' founding Board members (and I a rock "retiree").  Then again, given the generational distance, perhaps our genre is now considered "classical" and not hard(ly) rock to his "ears" (i.e., brain!)

Thanks again for taking the time to explain the application process and the positive response to our Letter of Intent.  I understand, of course, that your and Mrs Katz's intellectual interests in music, medicine, and science run orthogonal to the route through which we met. 

Re: you Global Programs - Might you know of other medical centers in the U.S. or around the world that might like to partner with us in a multi-center randomized controlled clinical trial on auditory enrichment in premature infants?

My colleagues and I look forward to uploading our LOI for future consideration in the months ahead.
